During this registration, you may come a: During this registration, you may come across a few specialist terms, which we have described below
Please contact your Local Council if you: Please contact your Local Council if you need to speak to someone about your food business registration urgently.
Find out more by visiting the: Find out more by visiting the
Food Standards Agency website (opens in: Food Standards Agency website (opens in new window)
Is there anything else you need to tell: Is there anything else you need to tell us about this establishment for example what type of food you are planning to sell and what food handling or processing methods you plan to use (optional)
What kind of food business are you regis: What kind of food business are you registering?
What type of water supply does this esta: What type of water supply does this establishment use?
An establishment is the location of your: An establishment is the location of your food business, and the food activities taking place there. If it is a mobile food business, please use the location where it is normally stored overnight.
The water supply is where you get your t: The water supply is where you get your tap water from. It has a private water supply if it uses water that you take up from the ground by yourself. For example from a well.
Both mains and private water supplies: Both mains and private water supplies
I don't know if I have a private water s: I don't know if I have a private water supply
If you are not registered with a water s: If you are not registered with a water supply company or paying a bill for water, this is an indication that your water supply could be private.
Please give us the details of the person: Please give us the details of the person at this company or charity we should speak to about food hygiene and safety.
What is a food business operator?: What is a food business operator?
The operator is the person or people, ch: The operator is the person or people, charity or company who makes the decisions about the food business. They decide what it serves and how it operates.
We will use your email to keep you infor: We will use your email to keep you informed of any policy or legal changes that could affect your food business.
Review these statements and tick all thr: Review these statements and tick all three boxes to agree.
I declare that the information I have gi: I declare that the information I have given on this form is correct and complete to the best of my knowledge and belief.
The operator will notify their local cou: The operator will notify their local council of any significant changes to the business activity, including closure, within 28 days of the change happening.
The operator understands they are legall: The operator understands they are legally responsible for the safety and authenticity of the food being produced or served at this establishment.
Congratulations, you've almost finished. We want to provide you with the best experience possible and value your feedback.
I agree to be contacted to provide feedb: I agree to be contacted to provide feedback to help develop this service (optional)

It is trading if it is already producing: It is trading if it is already producing or serving food, or if the new operator has already taken control

A trading name is what your customers wi: A trading name is what your customers will call your business.
What is the trading name of this establi: What is the trading name of this establishment?
When you start a new food business or ta: When you start a new food business or take over an existing business, you must register with your local authority. You should do this at least
before trading or before food operations: before trading or before food operations start.
The operator is the person, charity or c: The operator is the person, charity or company who makes the decisions about the food business. They decide what it serves and how it operates.
The establishment is the location or sit: The establishment is the location or site of your food business. If it is a mobile food business, please use the location where it is normally stored overnight.
is the local authority your registration: is the local authority your registration will be sent to. Is this the correct local authority for your business? If unsure please use this [Food Business Registration](https://www.gov.uk/food-business-registration) link to check using the location or site of your food business.

When will this establishment be open and: When will this establishment be open and serving or preparing food?
only serving food during December at Chr: only serving food during December at Christmas markets
open at weekends in June and August: open at weekends in June and August between 10:00 and 19:00
What days will this establishment be pro: What days will this establishment be producing or serving food?
What days will this establishment be ope: What days will this establishment be open and producing or serving food?
I don't know what days to select: I don't know what days to select
The food business will have irregular op: The food business will have irregular opening days if it isn't open the same days every week. This could include seasonal establishments such as summer ice-cream vendors. It can also include establishments which only open for events such as football matches.
Fill in the expected opening times for t: Fill in the expected opening times for this establishment using 24 hour clocks. For example, 09:30 to 19:00.
Details of the operating charity, organi: Details of the operating charity, organisation or trust
Charities that are registered with the C: Charities that are registered with the Charities Commission will have a charity reference number. You can find your charity reference number by visiting the
Charity Commission website (opens in new: Charity Commission website (opens in new window)
The name of the registered company that: The name of the registered company that is acting as the operator of this food business.
Every registered company will have a Com: Every registered company will have a Companies House reference number which will usually be either eight numbers (e.g. 12345678) or two letters followed by six numbers (e.g. AB123456)
I don't know my Companies House number: I don't know my Companies House number
Every limited company has to be register: Every limited company has to be registered with Companies House, and will have a company reference number. You can find your company reference number by visiting the
Companies House website (opens in new wi: Companies House website (opens in new window)

Establishments begin trading when they f: Establishments begin trading when they first start serving or manufacturing food or drink for customers or supplying food or drink online.
When is this establishment expected to b: When is this establishment expected to begin trading?
I don't know when this establishment wil: I don't know when this establishment will begin trading
Food businesses are required to register: Food businesses are required to register at least 28 days before they begin trading, that includes food or drink sold online. If you are not sure when this business will open, it may be too early to register. You can also use an estimated date.
What date did this establishment begin t: What date did this establishment begin trading?
I don't know when this establishment beg: I don't know when this establishment began trading
Trading begins the day your business sta: Trading begins the day your business started to serve or make food or drink. Alternatively, it can be the day you took over the business as an operator. If you do not remember the exact date, use an estimated date.
In a partnership, you and your partner (: In a partnership, you and your partner (or partners) personally share responsibility for your food business

Accessibility statement for 'Register a: Accessibility statement for 'Register a food business' service
This accessibility statement applies to: This accessibility statement applies to the Register a food business service.
Reporting accessibility problems with th: Reporting accessibility problems with this website
Technical information about this website: Technical information about this website's accessibility
Register a food business service (opens: Register a food business service (opens in a new tab)
is run by the Food Standards Agency. We: is run by the Food Standards Agency. We want as many people as possible to be able to use this service. For example, that means you should be able to:
zoom in up to 300% without the text spil: zoom in up to 300% without the text spilling off the screen
navigate most of the website using just: navigate most of the website using just a keyboard
navigate most of the website using speec: navigate most of the website using speech recognition software
listen to most of the website using a sc: listen to most of the website using a screen reader (including the most recent versions of JAWS, NVDA and VoiceOver)
We've also made the website text as simp: We've also made the website text as simple as possible to understand.
has advice on making your device easier: has advice on making your device easier to use if you have a disability.
We know some parts of this website are n: We know some parts of this website are not fully accessible. You can see a full list of any issues in the Non-accessible content section of this statement.
If you need information on this website: If you need information on this website in a different format like accessible PDF, large print, easy read, audio recording or braille:
Phone our Helpline: 0330 332 7149 (open: Phone our Helpline: 0330 332 7149 (open 9.00am until 5pm, Monday to Friday)
We will consider your request and get ba: We will consider your request and get back to you in 10 days.
We're always looking to improve the acce: We're always looking to improve the accessibility of this website. If you find any problems not listed on this page or think we're not meeting accessibility requirements, contact:
Read tips on contacting organisations ab: Read tips on contacting organisations about inaccessible websites (opens in a new tab)
The Equality and Human Rights Commission: The Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC) is responsible for enforcing the Public Sector Bodies (Websites and Mobile Applications) (No. 2) Accessibility Regulations 2018 (the 'accessibility regulations'). If you're not happy with how we respond to your complaint,
contact the Equality Advisory and Suppor: contact the Equality Advisory and Support Service (EASS) (opens in a new tab)
If you are in Northern Ireland and are n: If you are in Northern Ireland and are not happy with how we respond to your complaint you can contact the
Equalities Commission for Northern Irela: Equalities Commission for Northern Ireland (opens in a new tab)
who are responsible for enforcing the Pu: who are responsible for enforcing the Public Sector Bodies (Websites and Mobile Applications) (No. 2) Accessibility Regulations 2018 (the 'accessibility regulations') in Northern Ireland.
The Food Standards Agency is committed t: The Food Standards Agency is committed to making its website accessible, in accordance with the Public Sector Bodies (Websites and Mobile Applications) (No. 2) Accessibility Regulations 2018.
This website is partially compliant with: This website is partially compliant with the
Web Content Accessibility Guidelines ver: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines version 2.1 (opens in a new tab)
AA standard, due to the non-compliances: AA standard, due to the non-compliances and exemptions listed below.
The content listed below is non-accessib: The content listed below is non-accessible for the following reasons.
The content that is not accessible is ou: The content that is not accessible is outlined below with details of planned dates for when issues will be fixed.
Some links do not clearly explain their: Some links do not clearly explain their purpose or specify when they open in a new window. This could be confusing for screen reader users. We are working to contextualise these links and make them clearer. (WCAG 2.1 2.4.4 Link Purpose (In Context))
Some headings are not descriptive and do: Some headings are not descriptive and do not provide a clear indication as to what the page is about. Users are encouraged to read the page contents for full details. (WCAG 2.1 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels)
The table summary of user details are no: The table summary of user details are not hidden correctly when in high contrast mode. (WCAG 2.1 1.4.8 Visual presentation)
Some form fields are not grouped appropr: Some form fields are not grouped appropriately, require clearer field labels and group descriptions. Although, there are labels available they are not all clear enough to describe the content. This should not impact on a users ability to complete the form. (WCAG 2.1 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels)
Some form fields require greater context: Some form fields require greater context to ensure users can identify the purpose of the edit field. (WCAG 2.1 2.4.6 Headings and labels)
Some help text appears on pages that do: Some help text appears on pages that do not need that information, this could confuse users. Users are encouraged to ignore help text if it appears out of context. (WCAG 2.1 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions)
Visual focus indicators do not meet the: Visual focus indicators do not meet the minimum required colour contrast ratio. We are working to amend this as quickly as possible. (WCAG 2.1 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast)
Some fields have drop down options that: Some fields have drop down options that cannot be accessed when using the service with reflow options. This might inhibit a users ability to fill out the form, please try on a different device if reflow causes an issue. (WCAG 2.1 1.4.10 Reflow)
At this time, we have not made any dispr: At this time, we have not made any disproportionate burden claims.
Content that's not within the scope of t: Content that's not within the scope of the accessibility regulations
At this time, we have not identified any: At this time, we have not identified any content that is not within the scope of the accessibility regulations.
This statement was prepared on 11 Septem: This statement was prepared on 11 September 2020. It was last updated on 14 July 2021.
This service was last tested on 22 April: This service was last tested on 22 April 2021. The test was carried out by Digital Accessibility Centre (DAC). We will update this statement as soon as the issues have been rectified.
Register a food business privacy notice: Register a food business privacy notice
Information on our Register a food busin: Information on our Register a food business privacy notice, why we require data, what we do with the data and your rights.
What personal information will be collec: What personal information will be collected?
Purpose and lawful basis for processing: Purpose and lawful basis for processing
What we need and how we use it: What we need and how we use it
How and where we store your data and who: How and where we store your data and who we may share it with
The Food Standards Agency will be what i: The Food Standards Agency will be what is known as the 'Data Controller' of the personal data provided to us.
Personal identifying data collected as p: Personal identifying data collected as part of the Register a Food Business service may include:
name: name
We need to collect this information for: We need to collect this information for the purposes of fulfilling our statutory obligations, under Article 113 of the Official Controls Regulation (EU) 2017/625, and as retained in UK legislation, on official controls to make details of food businesses available to persons enquiring about the registration and/or approval status of food business operators.
We do this in line with the exercise of: We do this in line with the exercise of official authority vested in us and the performance of a task carried out in the public interest. We will not collect any personal data from you which we do not need.
We may want to contact you to invite you: We may want to contact you to invite you to provide feedback on the service. We will only do this with your consent.
The details of approved food business es: The details of approved food business establishments are published on the FSA website. The list of registered food business establishments is published by the relevant local authority.
We may also analyse this information alo: We may also analyse this information along with other information we hold about you and information we have obtained from public and/or private sources for the purpose of helping us evaluate risk. We do this in line with the exercise of official authority vested in us under the Food Standards Act and the performance of a task carried out in the public interest.
Where you provide your email address on: Where you provide your email address on the feedback form during your registration or agree to being contacted to provide feedback on the service at the end of the registration process, the FSA, or one of our research partners working on our behalf, may contact you for this purpose. You can withdraw your consent at any time by contacting registration@food.gov.uk
We retain personal information only for: We retain personal information only for as long as necessary to carry out these functions, and in line with our retention policy. This means this information will be retained for as long as a business remains registered and/or approved as a food business operator. Historical lists of registered and/or approved businesses are retained for 10 years.
No third parties have access to your per: No third parties have access to your personal data unless the law allows them to do so. In line with this commitment your information will be passed to the relevant local authority with responsibility for inspection of food businesses. The Food Standards Agency will sometimes share data with other government departments, public bodies, organisations which perform public functions to assist them in the performance of their statutory duties, and with these and other private organisations and persons, such as food business operators, when it is in the public interest. We may also share the data as part of risk evaluation and analysis with public bodies or other organisations, such as Trading Standards and Port Health Authorities, for the same reasons.

Your rights section in our Personal Info: Your rights section in our Personal Information Charter (opens in a new tab)
If you have any queries concerning this: If you have any queries concerning this Privacy Notice, your personal information or any questions on our use of the information, please email our Data Protection Officer in the FSA, who is the Information Management and Security Team Leader using the address below.
When we provide services, we want to mak: When we provide services, we want to make them easy, useful and reliable. Our website puts small files (known as 'cookies') onto your computer to collect information about how you browse the site.
measure how you use the website so it ca: measure how you use the website so it can be updated and improved based on your needs
remember the notifications you've seen s: remember the notifications you've seen so that we don't show them to you again
Cookies on this service are not used to: Cookies on this service are not used to identify you personally.
Used to persist your registration data a: Used to persist your registration data as you progress through the service
We use cookies to collect information ab: We use cookies to collect information about how you use this service. This helps us make sure the service is meeting your needs and helps us make improvements.
Helps us count how many people visit the: Helps us count how many people visit the service by tracking if you've visited before
You can change which cookies you're happ: You can change which cookies you're happy for us to use.
